latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

latest.content:

RNS Number : 7999C

09 October 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED (the "Company")
(a closed-ended investment company incorporated in Guernsey with registration number 60552)
Final Net Asset Value & Factsheet
Share ISIN: GG00BYRGPD65
Share SEDOL:
BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF
LEI: 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a small UK‑listed company on the AIM market.  Its public filings do not disclose a clear product line, but it appears to operate in a niche sector that has historically attracted a small, specialised investor base.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Even without a high‑profile business model, AIM‑listed firms often sit in high‑growth or low‑competition spaces.  The company’s limited visibility means it can move sharply if a new initiative or funding round materialises.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a maintenance phase, focusing on shareholder communication and capital management.  Trading activity is very light and the stock sits far below its historical peaks, indicating a steep discount but also a low‑profile status that can surprise when a catalyst arrives.

rns_narrative: Recent announcements are largely routine – updates on net asset value, AGM results, and shareholder holdings.
The company is mainly keeping its governance in order rather than pushing new products or earnings.
There is no evidence of a major strategic shift or new revenue source in the last few months.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent market – very little chatter or activity around the stock.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish posts have emerged; the silence is a contrarian signal that the market has overlooked the firm.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices are absent, but the lack of new information can be interpreted as stagnation or potential decline.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; no recent spikes in discussion or sentiment changes have been detected.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning there is no current search interest and a potential for surprise if a catalyst emerges.

near_term_catalysts: A capital‑raising event or a fresh funding round could inject liquidity and create a buying catalyst.
A new product launch or partnership announcement would provide a clear earnings signal.
A significant uptick in trading volume—say a 30–50% spike above the 20‑day average—could trigger a breakout.
Breaking above the most recent low support level with sustained volume would be a technical sign of a bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on any sudden change in the net asset value, a new RNS that outlines a strategic pivot, or a noticeable surge in share price or volume.  A sudden media mention could also act as a catalyst.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep slump with little recent activity.  Mean reversion could take several months, but the exact timing depends on whether a catalyst is announced.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the absence of clear catalysts and the lack of recent trading activity make it too early to commit to a bullish or bearish stance.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company announces new funding or a product launch, the steep discount relative to its past could translate into a 100 %+ upside as the price reverts toward previous levels.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without growth drivers, the firm may continue to trade at a discount or decline further if it fails to secure additional capital or generate revenue.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is large if a catalyst appears, but downside remains high due to the lack of fundamentals and the risk of continued stagnation.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout from the most recent low once volume rises noticeably.  Use a stop a few percent below that low and size the position for 10–15 % of the portfolio if bullish conviction grows.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a significant announcement—such as a new funding round or product launch—or for a 30–50 % volume spike.  Only consider entering once the price confirms a move above the recent support level.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks (delisting, bankruptcy) are evident, so the setup remains viable for a contrarian play.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A silent AIM listing trading at a very low level with no recent catalysts – a contrarian setup that may wait for a surprise event to trigger a mean‑reversion bounce, but the risk of continued stagnation is high.
